description: A medium sized tern about same size as nearby Common Tern, but with a smaller head, narrower wings and an overall daintier appearance than Common Tern.  Arctic's black cap extended to nape & top of crown. Forehead was white. Silvery white mantle showed no dark markings at all including none of the usual thin dark lines outlining primaries often seen on Arctic Terns. Primaries & outer secondaries appeared paler than rest of mantle suggesting 2nd cycle bird. Distance(closest view about 100 yards)may have prevented my seeing these lines. Underside of wings were paler than mantle with no dark markings nor lines observed. Bill was dark but distance made its size & shape difficult to assess. I did not see legs well.  Very distinctive were the bird's "jizz" & flight pattern. Its small head made the bird's center of gravity appear weighted to its rear, even though its forked white tail did not appear unusually long. Its flight pattern appeared somewhat labored with slight but rapid increases & decreases in elevation depending on respective downstroke & upstroke of wings, giving the flight pattern a more bouncy & buoyant pattern than nearby Common Tern's whose flight pattern was more flat-lined and gull like.Behaviors: I observed this bird only in flight as it circled and foraged around the lake occasionally diving to catch prey.Call: none heardPlumage: 2nd cyle or possibly winter adult
